items.text: Good morning, little explorers, Kenton and Eddey! Today is Monday, June twenty-second. Shanghai is still in the plum-rain season, so the morning is grey and cloudy, and a warm shower may drift past later in the day. The air is wet and a little sticky, about twenty-nine degrees, so a light, cool shirt is just right. Pop a small umbrella into your bag before you leave, just in case. Ready? Let's go and explore the big world together!
小探险家 Kenton、Eddey，早上好！今天是 6 月 22 日，星期一。上海还在梅雨季节里，早晨天空灰灰的、多云，白天晚些时候可能飘过一阵暖暖的雨。空气湿湿的、有点黏，气温大约 29 度，穿一件薄薄的、凉快的上衣正合适。出门前，记得往书包里塞一把小伞，以防万一。准备好了吗？我们一起去探索这个大世界吧！
Good news for everyone who loves the stars! This week the Earth gave us something special: the longest day of the whole year.
爱看星星的小朋友，好消息！这个星期，地球送给我们一份特别的礼物：一年里白天最长的一天。
It is called the summer solstice, and this year it happened on June twenty-first, the day before today.
这一天叫“夏至”，今年的夏至就在 6 月 21 日，也就是今天的前一天。
On that day, the Sun stayed up in the sky longer than on any other day, and the night was the shortest of the year.
在那一天，太阳待在天空里的时间，比一年里任何一天都长，而黑夜，是一年中最短的。
But why is one day longer than all the rest? The secret is hiding in the way our Earth leans.
可是，为什么会有一天比别的日子都长呢？秘密，就藏在地球“歪着身子”的样子里。
Our planet does not stand up straight. It is tilted a little, like a spinning top that leans to one side.
我们的地球并不是笔直站着的。它稍微斜着一点，就像一个转着转着、偏向一边的陀螺。
In June, the top half of the Earth, where we live, leans toward the Sun, so we get more sunlight and longer days.
到了六月，地球的上半边——也就是我们住的这一半——朝着太阳倾斜，所以我们得到更多阳光，白天也更长。
This same gentle lean is what gives us our seasons. When our half leans toward the Sun, it is summer, warm and bright.
正是这同一个轻轻的倾斜，给了我们四季。当我们这半边朝着太阳倾斜时，就是夏天，又暖又亮。
Six months later, our half leans away from the Sun, the days grow short, and cold winter comes.
再过六个月，我们这半边变成背对太阳，白天变短，寒冷的冬天就来了。
So summer and winter are not about being nearer to the Sun. They are all about which way the Earth is leaning.
所以，夏天和冬天，并不是因为离太阳更近或更远，而完全取决于地球朝着哪个方向倾斜。
Because the day was so long, the night of the solstice was also the shortest night of the whole year.
因为白天那么长，夏至那一晚，也就成了一整年里最短的夜晚。
From now on, day by day, the Sun will rise a tiny bit later, and each night will slowly grow longer again.
从现在开始，日子一天天过去，太阳会一天比一天升得晚一点点，黑夜也会慢慢重新变长。
People all around the world welcome this longest day with music, lights, and parties that last late into the evening.
全世界的人，都会用音乐、灯光和热闹的派对，来迎接这白天最长的一天，一直玩到很晚很晚。
Summer is also one of the very best times of the year to look up at the night sky.
夏天，也是一年里最适合抬头看夜空的时候之一。
On a clear, dark evening you can spot bright planets like Venus and Jupiter, shining steadily without even twinkling.
在一个晴朗、漆黑的夜晚，你能找到像金星、木星这样明亮的行星，它们稳稳地亮着，却几乎不眨眼。
You may also see a faint, cloudy band of stars stretching across the sky. That far-off band is part of our own galaxy, the Milky Way.
你说不定还会看到一条淡淡的、云雾般的星星带，横跨整个天空。那条遥远的带子，正是我们自己所在的星系——银河的一部分。
So tonight, if the rain clouds clear away, ask a grown-up to step outside with you and look up.
所以今天晚上，如果雨云散开了，就请大人陪你一起走到外面，抬头看看吧。
The same Sun that gave us the longest day will set, and one by one the stars will come out to say hello.
那个给了我们最长白天的太阳会落下，星星会一颗接一颗探出头来，跟你打招呼。
Our Earth is always spinning and leaning and travelling around the Sun, and that quiet dance gives us our days, our nights, and our seasons.
我们的地球一直在自转、倾斜，绕着太阳旅行，正是这场安静的舞蹈，给了我们白天、黑夜和四季。
solstice
至日(夏至/冬至)
tilt
倾斜
season
季节
planet
行星
galaxy
星系
sunlight
阳光
Our second story dives deep under the waves to meet one of the strangest and smartest animals in the whole ocean: the octopus.
第二条新闻，要潜到海浪深处，去认识整个海洋里最奇特、也最聪明的动物之一：章鱼。
An octopus has a soft, round body and no bones at all, so it can squeeze through a hole as small as a coin.
章鱼有着软软的、圆圆的身体，全身一根骨头都没有，所以它能从一个像硬币那么小的洞里挤过去。
And of course it has eight long arms, which is exactly what its name means: eight feet.
当然啦，它还有八条长长的手臂——这正是它名字的意思：“八只脚”。
Those eight arms are covered with rows of round suckers that can taste and grip at the very same time.
这八条手臂上，长满了一排排圆圆的吸盘，既能尝味道，又能牢牢抓住东西。
Here is something amazing: each arm can feel and even decide what to do almost on its own, as if the octopus had eight little helpers.
有件事特别神奇：每一条手臂几乎都能自己感觉、自己决定要做什么，就好像章鱼有八个小帮手一样。
With those clever arms it can open jars, build little shelters out of shells, and solve tricky puzzles in a tank.
靠着这些聪明的手臂，它能拧开罐子、用贝壳搭起藏身的小窝，还能解开水箱里刁钻的谜题。
An octopus is strange on the inside too. It does not have just one heart like you. It has three!
章鱼的身体里面，也一样奇怪。它不像你只有一颗心脏——它有三颗！
Two of the hearts push blood to its gills to gather oxygen, and the third heart sends blood around the rest of its body.
其中两颗心脏，把血液送到鳃里去收集氧气；第三颗心脏，则把血液送往身体的其它地方。
Even stranger, its blood is not red like ours. It is blue, which helps it live in the cold, dark water of the deep sea.
更奇怪的是，它的血不像我们的是红色的，而是蓝色的，这能帮它在又冷又黑的深海里生活。
But the octopus's greatest trick of all is hiding. In less than a second, it can change the colour of its whole body.
不过，章鱼最厉害的绝招是“藏”。不到一秒钟，它就能改变全身的颜色。
It can turn sandy yellow to match the seabed, or bumpy and brown to look just like a rock or a piece of coral.
它能变成沙子一样的黄色，融进海底；也能变得坑坑洼洼、棕棕的，看上去就像一块石头或一丛珊瑚。
It does this with tiny bags of colour in its skin, vanishing right in front of your eyes to hide from sharks and other hunters.
它靠皮肤里一个个小小的“颜色口袋”做到这一点，在你眼前一下子就消失，躲开鲨鱼和别的猎食者。
If a hungry enemy still manages to find it, the octopus has one more clever escape plan ready.
如果饿肚子的敌人还是找到了它，章鱼还准备着另一个聪明的逃跑计划。
It shoots out a cloud of dark ink that hangs in the water like smoke, and while the enemy is confused, it jets away.
它会喷出一团黑黑的墨汁，像烟雾一样悬在水里；趁敌人晕头转向的时候，它“嗖”地一下喷水逃走。
Scientists say the octopus is one of the smartest animals without a backbone, with a memory good enough to remember the people who feed it.
科学家说，章鱼是没有脊椎骨的动物里最聪明的之一，记性好到能记住经常喂它的人。
Octopuses live in every ocean on Earth, from warm and shallow rock pools to the cold, crushing deep sea.
章鱼生活在地球上的每一片海洋里，从温暖的浅水洼，到又冷又“压得人喘不过气”的深海。
Some are as tiny as your thumb, while the giant Pacific octopus can stretch out wider than a car.
有的章鱼小得像你的大拇指，而巨大的“北太平洋巨型章鱼”，张开来能比一辆汽车还宽。
So the sea is full of clever, gentle creatures that we are only just beginning to understand. The more we explore, the more wonders we find.
所以你看，大海里满是聪明又温柔、我们才刚刚开始了解的生灵。我们越去探索，就会发现越多的奇妙。
octopus
章鱼
sucker
吸盘
heart
心脏
camouflage
伪装, 隐蔽
ink
墨汁
ocean
海洋
